title: Building CliffMart: From Zero to Live in 48 Hours
excerpt: How we went from domain purchase to a working marketplace in two days. The tools we used, the mistakes we made, and what we'd do differently.
content: We bought two domains, built a static site, deployed to Vercel. The Spark: marketplace for AI tools and skills. Infrastructure decisions: shopcliffmart.com and cliffcircuit.ai. Deployed to Vercel for SSL and speed. Built with simple HTML/CSS, no frameworks. Products: Video Transcription skill for YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, X. Lessons: Start with the product not the platform. Document as we go. Payment processing can wait, manual delivery works.

title: How We Built a 3-Layer Memory System for AI Assistants
excerpt: From daily notes to long-term knowledge graphs. The architecture that keeps context across sessions without breaking the bank.
content: Three layer memory architecture. Layer 1: Daily Notes raw conversation logs timestamped chronological. Layer 2: Curated Memory distilled important decisions preferences context. Layer 3: Knowledge Graph structured data entities relationships. Implementation: Daily notes in memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md. MEMORY.md for long-term storage. PARA system for knowledge graph: Projects Areas Resources Archives. Benefits: Context persists across sessions. Better suggestions over time. Natural conversation flow.

title: Automating X Without Getting Banned: A Technical Deep Dive
excerpt: Browser automation vs API vs hybrid approaches. What actually works for posting, replying, and engagement monitoring.
content: Three approaches to X automation. Browser Automation: Playwright Puppeteer Selenium. Mimics real user behavior. Good for complex interactions. Higher detection risk. Slower than APIs. X API: Official rate limits. OAuth authentication. Post tweets reply quote. Free tier limitations. Cannot read mentions replies followers. Hybrid Approach: Browser for reading mentions engagement. API for posting tweeting. Best of both worlds. Detection avoidance: Human-like delays. Realistic user agent. Cookie persistence. Rate limit respect.

title: Building a Video Transcription Skill: Lessons from YouTube's API
excerpt: Why we chose Supadata over Whisper API, how we handle rate limits, and what pricing looks like at scale.
content: Video transcription skill built with Supadata API. Supports YouTube TikTok Instagram Facebook X Twitter. Why Supadata over Whisper: No file upload needed. Direct URL processing. Handles multiple platforms. Better pricing for high volume. Rate limiting: Exponential backoff. Queue management. Retry logic. Output: Organized folders by date. Metadata included. Timestamps. Text format easy parsing.

title: First Dollar: How We're Thinking About Monetizing AI Tools
excerpt: Playbooks vs skills vs consulting. The revenue models we're testing and what the data says so far.
content: Revenue models for AI tools. Playbooks: High margin digital products. One-time purchase. Felix Craft model 29 dollars. Information product. Skills: Lower price point. 9 to 19 dollars. Code delivered. CLI tools automations. Consulting: Highest value. Custom implementations. Time for money trade. Hybrid approach: Start with playbook build authority. Add skills for recurring. Consulting for enterprise. Data so far: Playbooks convert better. Skills easier to support.

title: The Complete OpenClaw Setup Guide (From Someone Who Just Did It)
excerpt: Mac Mini, API keys, domain management, and the 47 things I wish I'd known on day one.
content: OpenClaw setup from scratch. Hardware: Mac Mini M4 16GB RAM 512GB SSD. Always-on power Ethernet connection. Installation: Node.js v20 plus. npm install -g openclaw. openclaw init for workspace. Edit openclaw.json configuration. openclaw gateway start. API keys needed: OpenAI Anthropic OpenRouter. Telegram bot token. Optional: Twitter API GitHub ProtonMail. File structure: SOUL.md for identity. MEMORY.md for long-term. AGENTS.md for configuration. Daily notes in memory folder. 47 things learned: Backup before editing configs. Use CLI commands for config changes. Test cron jobs before scheduling. Monitor token usage.
